notes: DDR5-6400 remained anchored by 64GB and 128GB, with 96GB repaired but 32GB still lacking a stronger fresh exact public anchor.
DDR5-5600 stayed structurally split at 32GB across low-retail and high-distributor bands; this remains outlier_only rather than no_public_sample.
PCIe4 TLC remained the cleanest and most executable category, with 30.72TB public coverage holding after the earlier recovery.
PCIe5 TLC nominal coverage remained acceptable, but 30.72TB still failed to recover the older lower-band anchor cleanly and continues to show stale/family split behaviour.
PCIe4 QLC stayed fully covered, while high-capacity supplementation still leaned on parser-gap and anti-bot classification at Mouser, PCNation, Connection, and some direct product pages.

noDirectSampleSources: 16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
outlierOnlySources: A-Tech/Newegg/CDW Kingston low cluster vs Provantage/CDW/Connection high cluster for 32GB

noDirectSampleSources: lower-band exact 30.72TB public anchor still missing

keyChangesVsYesterday: No tracked representative value changed versus 2026-04-25.

Today’s added work mainly strengthened parser_gap vs spec-only vs stale classification across SHI, Tech-America, Insight, Connection, Mouser, and PCNation rather than adding fresh representative repricing.
DDR5-5600 32GB still did not converge into a unified representative public band and remains outlier_only.
PCIe5 TLC 30.72TB still did not recover a trustworthy lower-band anchor; today’s added checks mainly ruled out false recovery paths.

DDR5-6400.reason: 64GB and 128GB remain stable, 96GB remains visible after the repair, but 32GB still lacks a stronger clean exact anchor and 16GB is still absent.

DDR5-5600.reason: 32GB remains structurally split across low retail and high distributor bands, while 64GB and 128GB remain visible but not at attractive low levels.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: This remains the most stable and fully covered category, with repeatable public distribution bands and no new sign of anchor decay.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: Lower capacities remain observable, but 30.72TB still lacks a trustworthy single representative public band because the old lower anchor remains stale while cleaner replacements did not materialize.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Main premium public bands remain usable, but high-capacity low clusters and parser-gated supplementation remain too noisy to justify a one-shot bottom-call.

gaps.note: Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Low-side public pages remain far below the higher distributor band, and today's checks still did not produce a unified representative public band.

The previously preferred lower-band anchor still did not repeat cleanly; today's added checks mostly strengthened high-band or parser/spec-only interpretation instead of recovering a cleaner lower anchor.
The pages again returned denied, Cloudflare-gated, or access-denied content, confirming access/parser failure rather than true absence of public market pricing.
